LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, DEFENDANT BDO USA, LLP'S DEMURRER TO ALL FOUR CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF PATRICK CLANCY IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BEACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT, AND SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL AND WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR# 7489, MGHENO@SFTC.ORG, REPORTED. (D302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-19-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Katharine Liao, Esq. 310-595-3114 of DLA Piper US, LLP Los Angeles for Defendant through Court Call.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BDO USA, LLP's DEMURRER PLAINTIFF PATRICK CLANCY'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant BDO USA, LLP's demurrer to all four causes of action in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Patrick Clancy is overruled as to the first cause of action for breach of contract, sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for beach of the implied covenant, and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the third cause of action for promissory estoppel and wrongful termination in violation of public policy. While there is no case law on point, the legislative history of Labor Code 2751, former Labor Code 2752 and similar statutes in other states show that section 2751 is not intended to bar an employee from suing for commissions that his employer agreed to pay him in an oral contract, but is intended to impose the requirement on an employer to put the agreement in writing subject to any penalties or consequences provided in the Labor Code on the employer for not doing so. Section 2751 is employee protective legislation and thus it would be contrary to that section to be invoked to deny an employee of compensation that his employer agreed to pay him. The contract claims adequately alleges the terms of the commission contract. The implied covenant claim fails, as Mr. Clancy apparently recognizes, because it alleges the same breach of contract as the contract claim. The promissory estoppel claim fails to allege what Mr. Clancy did or did not do in reliance on BDO's alleged promise. The wrongful termination claim fails to clearly allege a nexus between the termination of Mr. Clancy's employment and conduct by BDO that violates a public policy expressed in a constitutional or statutory provision. Because it is reasonably possible that Mr. Clancy may be able to remedy the deficiencies in his promissory estoppel and wrongful termination claims, he is given leave to do so.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR# 7489, mgheno@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Michael Welch of the law offices of Michael Welch (415-399-1500) appears as counsel for plaintiff/non-opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT BDO USA, LLP's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the two causes of action in plaintiff Patrick Clancy's first amended complaint is SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend. In lieu of filing a substantive opposition to this demurrer, Mr. Clancy requested leave to file an amended pleading, which is granted, per California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR No. 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)
